name: Payload Center of Mass System Identification
description:
A robotics dynamics engineer must design an automated 'Load Identification' routine. When an industrial robot picks up a custom payload of unknown weight and distribution, the controller needs its mass and Center of Mass (CoM) to compute accurate dynamic feed-forward torques for high-speed motion.

Using the ZMQ Remote API in CoppeliaSim, build a simulation rig consisting of a static kinematic base, a force/torque sensor, and a dynamic payload with an asymmetrical mass and local position offset. Estimate the payload's mass and 3D CoM offset by recording steady-state 6-axis force and torque data across at least three distinct, orthogonal orientations in stepping mode. 

Save the recorded measurements to /home/ga/Documents/CoppeliaSim/exports/ft_measurements.csv (containing columns: pose_id, roll_deg, pitch_deg, yaw_deg, fx_n, fy_n, fz_n, tx_nm, ty_nm, tz_nm) and the final identification results to /home/ga/Documents/CoppeliaSim/exports/payload_identification.json (containing fields: total_orientations, true_mass_kg, true_com_x_m, true_com_y_m, true_com_z_m, estimated_mass_kg, estimated_com_x_m, estimated_com_y_m, estimated_com_z_m).

Login: username=ga, password=password123.
